title: Chapter II.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: Then Manes made the following reply to him: And what manner of God now is it that blinds one?

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: For it is Paul who uses these words: “In whom the God of this world hath blinded the minds of them which believe not, lest the light of the Gospel should shine in them.”

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: But Archelaus broke in and refuted this very well, saying: Read, however, a word or two of what precedes that sentence, namely, “But if our Gospel be hid, it is hid in them that are lost.”

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: You see that it is hid in them that are lost.

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: “For it is not meet to give the holy things to dogs.”

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: And furthermore, is it only the God of the Old Testament that has blinded the minds of them who believe not?

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: Nay, has not Jesus Himself also said: “Therefore speak I to them in parables: that seeing, they may not see?”

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: Is it then because He hated them that He desired them not to see?

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: Or is it not on account of their unworthiness, since they closed their own eyes?

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: For wherever wickedness is a matter self-chosen, there too there is the absence of grace.

verses.book: A Fragment of the Same Disputation.
verses.text: “For unto him that hath shall be given, but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he seemeth to have.”
